rawText:
It used to be that if you wanted to get a computer to do something new, you would have to program it. Now, programming, for those of you here that haven't done it yourself, requires laying out in excruciating detail every single step that you want the computer to do in order to achieve your goal. Now, if you want to do something that you don't know how to do yourself, then this is going to be a great challenge.

So this was the challenge faced by this man, Arthur Samuel. In 1956, he wanted to get this computer to be able to beat him at checkers. How can you write a program, lay out in excruciating detail, how to be better than you at checkers? So he came up with an idea: he had the computer play against itself thousands of times and learn how to play checkers. And indeed it worked, and in fact, by 1962, this computer had beaten the Connecticut state champion.

So Arthur Samuel was the father of machine learning, and I have a great debt to him, because I am a machine learning practitioner. I was the president of Kaggle, a community of over 200,000 machine learning practitioners. Kaggle puts up competitions to try and get them to solve previously unsolved problems, and it's been successful hundreds of times. So from this vantage point, I was able to find out a lot about what machine learning can do in the past, can do today, and what it could do in the future.

Perhaps the first big success of machine learning commercially was Google. Google showed that it is possible to find information by using a computer algorithm, and this algorithm is based on machine learning. Since that time, there have been many commercial successes of machine learning. Companies like Amazon and Netflix use machine learning to suggest products that you might like to buy, movies that you might like to watch. Sometimes, it's almost creepy. Companies like LinkedIn and Facebook sometimes will tell you about who your friends might be and you have no idea how it did it, and this is because it's using the power of machine learning. These are algorithms that have learned how to do this from data rather than being programmed by hand.

This is also how IBM was successful in getting Watson to beat the two world champions at "Jeopardy," answering incredibly subtle and complex questions like this one.

This is also why we are now able to see the first self-driving cars. If you want to be able to tell the difference between, say, a tree and a pedestrian, well, that's pretty important. We don't know how to write those programs by hand, but with machine learning, this is now possible. And in fact, this car has driven over a million miles without any accidents on regular roads.

overview.title: The Wonderful and Terrifying Implications of Computers That Can Learn

sentences.original: It used to be that if you wanted to get a computer to do something new, you would have to program it.
sentences.translation: 예전에는 컴퓨터에 새로운 일을 시키려면 프로그래밍을 해야 했습니다.

grammarElements.text: if you wanted to get a computer to do something new
grammarElements.label: 조건절 (if)

grammarElements.text: you would have to program it
grammarElements.label: 주절 (would have to — 과거의 필요)

vocabulary.example: I used to live in Seoul.
She learned to program in Python.

expressions.usage: 과거의 일반적 상황이 현재와 다름을 설명할 때

expressions.phrase: get a computer to do something
expressions.usage: get + 목적어 + to부정사로 사역의 뉘앙스 표현

patterns.pattern: It used to be that if [condition], [subject] would have to [verb]
patterns.explanation: 과거의 상태/조건을 설명하고, 현재는 달라졌음을 암시하는 도입 패턴.
examples: It used to be that if you wanted to send a message, you would have to write a letter.
It used to be that if you needed information, you would have to go to a library.

drillQuestions.question: It used _____ be that computers needed programming.
If you wanted to get a computer to do something, you _____ have to program it.

sentences.notes: "It used to be that"은 과거 상황을 설명하며 현재와의 대비를 암시하는 강연 오프닝 기법. "get + 목적어 + to부정사"는 사역 구문으로, make보다 부드러운 표현이다.

koreanAlignment.en: if you wanted to get a computer to do something new
you would have to program it

pronunciationNotes: used to: /juːst tə/ — "유스트 투", "유즈드 투"가 아님에 주의. d가 t 앞에서 묵음화
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 시간 대비(temporal contrast) — "예전에는"으로 시작하여 현재와의 변화를 암시하는 오프닝

sentences.original: Now, programming, for those of you here that haven't done it yourself, requires laying out in excruciating detail every single step that you want the computer to do in order to achieve your goal.
sentences.translation: 프로그래밍은, 여기 계신 분들 중 직접 해보지 않으신 분들을 위해 말씀드리면, 목표를 달성하기 위해 컴퓨터가 해야 할 모든 단계를 극도로 세밀하게 나열하는 것을 필요로 합니다.

grammarElements.text: for those of you here that haven't done it yourself
grammarElements.label: 삽입구 (청중 한정)

grammarElements.text: laying out in excruciating detail every single step
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (동명사구)

grammarElements.text: that you want the computer to do
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (that — step을 수식)

grammarElements.text: in order to achieve your goal
grammarElements.label: 목적 부사구

vocabulary.example: The process was described in excruciating detail.
She laid out her plan step by step.
She achieved her goal of graduating with honors.

expressions.phrase: for those of you here that
expressions.meaning: 여기 계신 분들 중 ~한 분들을 위해
expressions.usage: 청중 중 특정 그룹에게 설명을 한정할 때. TED 강연에서 흔한 청중 참여 기법
매우 상세하고 힘든 수준의 세밀함을 강조할 때
목적을 명시적으로 표현. to만 쓰는 것보다 공식적

patterns.pattern: [Subject] requires [verb-ing] in [adjective] detail every [noun] that ...
patterns.explanation: 어떤 과정이 얼마나 세밀한 작업을 요구하는지 설명하는 패턴.
examples: Engineering requires planning in precise detail every component that goes into the system.
Teaching requires preparing in careful detail every lesson that students need.

drillQuestions.question: Programming requires _____ out every single step.
You need to describe everything in excruciating _____.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 삽입구(for those of you...)로 인해 주어(programming)와 동사(requires)가 멀리 떨어져 있다. excruciating은 원래 "고통스러운"이라는 뜻이지만, 여기서는 "극도로 세밀한"이라는 의미로 사용되어 프로그래밍의 번거로움을 유머러스하게 표현한다.

koreanAlignment.en: for those of you here that haven't done it yourself
koreanAlignment.ko: 직접 해보지 않으신 분들을 위해 말씀드리면

koreanAlignment.en: every single step that you want the computer to do
in order to achieve your goal

sentences.original: Now, if you want to do something that you don't know how to do yourself, then this is going to be a great challenge.
sentences.translation: 자, 만약 여러분 스스로도 어떻게 하는지 모르는 것을 하고 싶다면, 이것은 큰 도전이 될 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: if you want to do something that you don't know how to do yourself
grammarElements.label: 조건절 (if + 관계대명사절)

grammarElements.text: then this is going to be
grammarElements.label: 주절 (be going to — 미래)

vocabulary.example: Learning a new language is a great challenge.

expressions.phrase: don't know how to do yourself
expressions.usage: 본인의 능력 범위 밖의 일을 지칭할 때
구어적 미래 표현. will보다 더 자연스러운 대화체

patterns.pattern: If you want to do something that you don't know how to do, then ...
patterns.explanation: 능력의 한계를 조건으로 설정하고 그 결과를 제시하는 패턴. 문제 상황을 설정하는 데 사용.
examples: If you want to cook something that you don't know how to cook, then you need a recipe.
If you want to fix something that you don't understand, then you need an expert.

drillQuestions.question: If you don't know how to do it _____, this is a great challenge.
This is going _____ be a great challenge.

sentences.notes: "something that you don't know how to do yourself"는 관계대명사절 안에 의문사 + to부정사(how to do)가 포함된 구조. 이 문장은 머신러닝의 핵심 동기를 설명하는 중요한 전환점이다.

koreanAlignment.en: if you want to do something
that you don't know how to do yourself
then this is going to be a great challenge

sentences.original: So this was the challenge faced by this man, Arthur Samuel.
sentences.translation: 그래서 이것이 바로 이 사람, 아서 새뮤얼이 직면한 도전이었습니다.

grammarElements.text: the challenge faced by this man
grammarElements.label: 보어 (과거분사구 후치수식)

vocabulary.example: We face many challenges in the future.

expressions.usage: 과거분사를 사용한 후치수식으로 주어를 한정

patterns.pattern: This was the [noun] faced by [person]
patterns.explanation: 이전에 언급한 문제를 특정 인물과 연결하는 전환 패턴. 스토리텔링의 핵심 기법.
examples: This was the dilemma faced by the scientists.
This was the question faced by every educator.

drillQuestions.question: This was the challenge _____ by Arthur Samuel.

sentences.notes: "faced by this man"은 과거분사구로 challenge를 뒤에서 수식. "this man, Arthur Samuel"에서 Arthur Samuel은 this man과 동격. 청중의 시선을 슬라이드의 인물로 유도하는 프레젠테이션 기법.

sentences.original: In 1956, he wanted to get this computer to be able to beat him at checkers.
sentences.translation: 1956년에, 그는 이 컴퓨터가 체커 게임에서 자신을 이길 수 있게 만들고 싶었습니다.

grammarElements.text: to be able to beat him at checkers
grammarElements.label: 목적격 보어 (to부정사)

vocabulary.example: She beat the champion in the final round.
Checkers is a classic strategy board game.

expressions.phrase: get something to be able to
expressions.usage: get + 목적어 + to부정사의 사역 구조에 be able to를 결합
게임이나 경쟁에서 상대를 이길 때

patterns.pattern: [Subject] wanted to get [object] to be able to [verb]
patterns.explanation: 목적어가 특정 능력을 갖추기를 원하는 복합 사역 구조.
examples: She wanted to get the robot to be able to walk.
They wanted to get the AI to be able to understand speech.

drillQuestions.question: He wanted to get the computer to be able to _____ him at checkers.
He wanted to get this computer _____ be able to play.

sentences.notes: "get + 목적어 + to부정사"의 사역 구조가 "be able to + 동사원형"과 결합된 복합 구조. checkers는 미국 영어이고, 영국에서는 draughts라고 부른다.

koreanAlignment.en: he wanted to get this computer
to be able to beat him
koreanAlignment.ko: 자신을 이길 수 있게 만들고 싶었습니다

pronunciationNotes: nineteen fifty-six: 연도는 두 자리씩 끊어 읽음

sentences.original: How can you write a program, lay out in excruciating detail, how to be better than you at checkers?
sentences.translation: 체커에서 당신보다 더 잘하는 방법을, 어떻게 극도로 세밀하게 나열하여 프로그램을 작성할 수 있을까요?

grammarElements.text: how to be better than you at checkers
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (의문사 + to부정사)

vocabulary.example: He laid out his argument clearly.

expressions.usage: 수사적 의문문으로, 불가능에 가까움을 암시
비교급 + than + at으로 특정 분야에서의 비교

patterns.pattern: How can you [verb] ... [how to verb] ...?
patterns.explanation: 수사적 의문문으로 특정 과제의 어려움을 부각하는 패턴.
examples: How can you teach a machine how to be creative?
How can you explain in detail how to recognize a face?

drillQuestions.question: How _____ you write a program to beat you at checkers?
He laid out the steps in excruciating _____.

sentences.notes: 수사적 의문문(rhetorical question)으로, 실제 대답을 기대하는 것이 아니라 "이것은 불가능에 가깝다"라는 것을 청중이 스스로 깨닫게 하는 기법. excruciating detail이 앞 문장에서 반복되어 강조 효과.

koreanAlignment.en: How can you write a program
how to be better than you at checkers

pronunciationNotes: excruciating: /ɪkˈskruːʃieɪtɪŋ/ — 반복 등장. 강연에서 의도적 반복
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 수사적 의문(rhetorical question) — 대답이 불가능한 질문으로 머신러닝의 필요성을 부각

sentences.original: So he came up with an idea: he had the computer play against itself thousands of times and learn how to play checkers.
sentences.translation: 그래서 그는 아이디어를 생각해냈습니다: 컴퓨터가 스스로 수천 번 대결하면서 체커 하는 법을 배우게 한 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: he had the computer play against itself
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 설명절 (사역동사 had)

grammarElements.text: and learn how to play checkers
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (and)

vocabulary.example: She came up with a brilliant solution.
The team played against the champions.

expressions.usage: 해결책을 발견한 순간을 이야기할 때
사역동사 have + 목적어 + 동사원형

patterns.pattern: [Subject] came up with an idea: [subject] had [object] [verb] ...
patterns.explanation: 문제 해결의 아이디어를 콜론으로 도입하고, 사역동사로 실행을 설명하는 스토리텔링 패턴.
examples: She came up with an idea: she had the students teach each other.
They came up with a plan: they had the robots work in teams.

drillQuestions.question: He came _____ with a brilliant idea.
He had the computer _____ against itself thousands of times.

sentences.notes: 사역동사 had + 목적어(the computer) + 동사원형(play)에 주목. make/let과 마찬가지로 have 뒤에는 동사원형이 온다. "play against itself"는 자기 자신과 대결한다는 의미로, 강화학습(reinforcement learning)의 초기 개념.

koreanAlignment.en: he came up with an idea
he had the computer play against itself
and learn how to play checkers

sentences.original: And indeed it worked, and in fact, by 1962, this computer had beaten the Connecticut state champion.
sentences.translation: 그리고 실제로 성공했습니다. 사실, 1962년에 이 컴퓨터는 코네티컷 주 챔피언을 이겼습니다.

vocabulary.example: The results were indeed impressive.
She became the world champion in tennis.

expressions.usage: 예상이 실현되었음을 강조할 때
추가적인 놀라운 정보를 제시할 때

patterns.pattern: And indeed it worked, and in fact, by [year], [subject] had [past participle] ...
patterns.explanation: 아이디어의 성공을 확인하고, 구체적 성과를 시간과 함께 제시하는 패턴.
examples: And indeed it worked, and in fact, by 2020, the company had reached 1 million users.
And indeed it succeeded, and in fact, by the next year, the project had won an award.

drillQuestions.question: And _____ it worked perfectly.
By 1962, the computer _____ beaten the state champion.

sentences.notes: 과거완료 "had beaten"은 1962년이라는 과거 시점보다 더 이전에 완료된 사건을 나타낸다. by + 시간은 "~까지"라는 의미로, 완료 시점의 한계를 설정.

sentences.original: So Arthur Samuel was the father of machine learning, and I have a great debt to him, because I am a machine learning practitioner.
sentences.translation: 아서 새뮤얼은 머신러닝의 아버지였고, 저는 그에게 큰 빚이 있습니다. 왜냐하면 저는 머신러닝 실무자이기 때문입니다.

grammarElements.text: and I have a great debt to him
grammarElements.label: 등위절

grammarElements.text: because I am a machine learning practitioner
grammarElements.label: 이유절 (because)

vocabulary.example: She is a medical practitioner.
I owe a debt of gratitude to my teacher.
Machine learning is transforming many industries.

expressions.usage: 특정 분야를 창시하거나 개척한 인물을 지칭
누군가에게 감사의 마음을 은유적으로 표현

patterns.pattern: [Person] was the father of [field], and I have a great debt to [person], because ...
patterns.explanation: 역사적 인물을 소개하고 자신과의 관계를 밝히는 자기소개 패턴.
examples: Turing was the father of computer science, and I have a great debt to him, because I am a programmer.

drillQuestions.question: Arthur Samuel was the _____ of machine learning.
I have a great _____ to him.

sentences.notes: "the father of"는 분야의 창시자를 의미하는 관용적 은유. "debt"는 물질적 빚이 아닌 학문적 은혜/감사의 은유적 표현. 여기서 화자(Jeremy Howard)가 자신의 전문성을 소개하며 신뢰도를 확보하는 수사 전략.

koreanAlignment.en: Arthur Samuel was the father of machine learning
I have a great debt to him
because I am a machine learning practitioner

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 권위 확립(ethos) — 역사적 인물과의 연결 및 자신의 전문성 소개로 신뢰도 확보

sentences.original: I was the president of Kaggle, a community of over 200,000 machine learning practitioners.
sentences.translation: 저는 200,000명 이상의 머신러닝 실무자 커뮤니티인 Kaggle의 회장이었습니다.

grammarElements.text: a community of over 200,000 machine learning practitioners
grammarElements.label: 동격 (Kaggle을 설명)

vocabulary.example: She was elected president of the organization.
The online community grew rapidly.

expressions.usage: 조직의 규모를 구체적 숫자로 제시할 때

patterns.pattern: I was the [position] of [organization], a [description].
patterns.explanation: 직함과 조직을 소개하고 동격으로 설명을 추가하는 자기소개 패턴.
examples: I was the CEO of a startup, a company focused on AI research.
She was the director of the lab, a team of 50 scientists.

drillQuestions.question: I was the president _____ Kaggle.
Kaggle is a community of _____ 200,000 practitioners.

sentences.notes: Kaggle과 동격인 "a community of over 200,000 machine learning practitioners"는 청중에게 익숙하지 않을 수 있는 고유명사를 설명하는 기법. over + 숫자는 "~이상"이라는 의미.

koreanAlignment.en: I was the president of Kaggle

sentences.original: Kaggle puts up competitions to try and get them to solve previously unsolved problems, and it's been successful hundreds of times.
sentences.translation: Kaggle은 이전에 풀리지 않은 문제들을 풀도록 경쟁을 개최하며, 수백 번 성공해왔습니다.

grammarElements.text: to try and get them to solve previously unsolved problems
grammarElements.label: 목적 부사구 (to부정사)

grammarElements.text: and it's been successful hundreds of times
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (현재완료)

vocabulary.example: The school put up a science competition.
This problem was previously considered unsolvable.
There are many unsolved problems in mathematics.

expressions.usage: "try to get them to"와 같은 의미. 구어적 표현
빈도의 크기를 강조하는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Organization] [verb] ... to solve previously unsolved [noun], and it's been successful ...
patterns.explanation: 조직의 활동과 성과를 소개하는 패턴. 현재완료로 지속적 성과를 강조.
examples: The lab conducts research to solve previously unsolved diseases, and it's been successful many times.

drillQuestions.question: Kaggle puts _____ competitions for machine learning.
It's been successful _____ of times.

sentences.notes: "try and get"은 "try to get"의 구어적 변형. 문법적으로는 "try to"가 정확하지만, 실제 대화와 강연에서는 "try and"가 매우 흔하다. previously unsolved는 un- 접두사(부정) + solved(풀린)의 조합.

koreanAlignment.en: to try and get them to solve
it's been successful hundreds of times

sentences.original: So from this vantage point, I was able to find out a lot about what machine learning can do in the past, can do today, and what it could do in the future.
sentences.translation: 그래서 이러한 유리한 관점에서, 저는 머신러닝이 과거에 무엇을 할 수 있었는지, 오늘날 무엇을 할 수 있는지, 그리고 미래에 무엇을 할 수 있을지에 대해 많이 알아낼 수 있었습니다.

grammarElements.text: a lot about what machine learning can do in the past
grammarElements.label: 목적어 1 (간접의문문)

grammarElements.text: and what it could do in the future
grammarElements.label: 목적어 3 (간접의문문)

vocabulary.example: From this vantage point, we can see the entire valley.
I found out the truth about the project.

expressions.usage: 자신의 경험이나 위치 덕분에 특별한 시각을 가질 수 있었음을 표현
could와 달리 실제로 성공적으로 수행했음을 나타냄

patterns.pattern: From this vantage point, I was able to [verb] a lot about what [X] can do ...
patterns.explanation: 특정 경험이나 위치가 제공하는 통찰력을 설명하는 패턴. 과거, 현재, 미래를 병렬로 나열.
examples: From this vantage point, I was able to learn a lot about what technology can do for education.

drillQuestions.question: From this vantage _____, I was able to see everything.
I was _____ to find out a lot about machine learning.

sentences.notes: "can do in the past"는 문법적으로는 "could do"가 자연스럽지만, 화자가 의도적으로 can을 사용하여 과거-현재-미래의 병렬 구조를 강조. vantage point는 원래 물리적인 "전망이 좋은 장소"이지만 여기서는 은유적으로 "유리한 관점"을 의미.

koreanAlignment.en: I was able to find out a lot
about what machine learning can do in the past
koreanAlignment.ko: 머신러닝이 과거에 무엇을 할 수 있었는지

koreanAlignment.en: and what it could do in the future

sentences.original: Perhaps the first big success of machine learning commercially was Google.
sentences.translation: 아마도 머신러닝의 첫 번째 상업적 대성공은 구글이었을 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: the first big success of machine learning commercially
grammarElements.label: 주어

vocabulary.example: The product was commercially successful.
Perhaps we should reconsider.

expressions.usage: 특정 분야에서 최초의 중요한 성과를 소개할 때

patterns.pattern: Perhaps the first big success of [field] was [example].
patterns.explanation: 분야의 역사적 이정표를 소개하는 패턴. perhaps로 단정을 피하면서 주장.
examples: Perhaps the first big success of social media was MySpace.
Perhaps the first big success of streaming was Netflix.

drillQuestions.question: _____ the first big success of machine learning was Google.
The first big success of machine learning _____ was Google.

sentences.notes: commercially는 문장 끝이 아닌 주어 내에 위치하여 "상업적으로의 성공"이라는 의미를 한정. perhaps는 절대적 주장을 피하는 학술적/강연 화법의 전형적 장치.

koreanAlignment.en: the first big success of machine learning commercially

sentences.original: Google showed that it is possible to find information by using a computer algorithm, and this algorithm is based on machine learning.
sentences.translation: 구글은 컴퓨터 알고리즘을 사용하여 정보를 찾는 것이 가능하다는 것을 보여주었고, 이 알고리즘은 머신러닝에 기반합니다.

grammarElements.text: that it is possible to find information
grammarElements.label: that절 (목적어)

grammarElements.text: and this algorithm is based on machine learning
grammarElements.label: 등위절

vocabulary.example: The search algorithm sorts results by relevance.
The movie is based on a true story.

expressions.phrase: showed that it is possible to
expressions.usage: 이전에 불가능하다고 여겨졌던 것의 실현을 증명할 때
수단/방법을 설명하는 전치사 + 동명사 구조

patterns.pattern: [Subject] showed that it is possible to [verb] by using [tool/method]
patterns.explanation: 특정 도구/방법을 통해 가능성을 증명한 사례를 소개하는 패턴.
examples: Tesla showed that it is possible to build electric cars by using advanced batteries.
The team showed that it is possible to detect cancer by using AI analysis.

drillQuestions.question: Google showed that it _____ possible to find information.
This algorithm is _____ on machine learning.

sentences.notes: "it is possible to"에서 it은 가주어, to find information이 진주어. "by using"은 by + 동명사 구조로 수단을 나타낸다. algorithm은 기술 분야의 핵심 용어로, 일상 영어에서도 점점 많이 사용되고 있다.

koreanAlignment.en: it is possible to find information
and this algorithm is based on machine learning

sentences.original: Companies like Amazon and Netflix use machine learning to suggest products that you might like to buy, movies that you might like to watch.
sentences.translation: 아마존이나 넷플릭스 같은 회사들은 여러분이 구매하고 싶어할 만한 제품이나, 시청하고 싶어할 만한 영화를 추천하기 위해 머신러닝을 사용합니다.

grammarElements.text: that you might like to buy
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (that — products를 수식)

grammarElements.text: movies that you might like to watch
grammarElements.label: 동격/병렬 (products와 병렬)

vocabulary.example: The app suggests restaurants based on your location.

expressions.usage: 대표적인 예시를 들 때
불확실한 선호를 정중하게 표현

patterns.pattern: Companies like [A] and [B] use [technology] to [verb] [things] that you might like to [verb]
patterns.explanation: 기술의 실제 활용 사례를 구체적 기업 예시와 함께 설명하는 패턴.
examples: Companies like Spotify and Apple use AI to recommend songs that you might like to listen to.

drillQuestions.question: Netflix uses machine learning to _____ movies.
Products that you _____ like to buy.

sentences.notes: "products that you might like to buy, movies that you might like to watch"는 병렬 구조로, 같은 문법 패턴을 반복하여 리듬감을 만든다. might like to는 may want to보다 부드럽고 정중한 표현.

koreanAlignment.en: to suggest products that you might like to buy
koreanAlignment.ko: 구매하고 싶어할 만한 제품을 추천하기 위해

koreanAlignment.en: movies that you might like to watch

sentences.original: Sometimes, it's almost creepy.
sentences.translation: 가끔은, 거의 소름끼칠 정도입니다.

vocabulary.example: That old house looks creepy at night.

expressions.usage: 기술의 정확도가 불편할 정도로 높을 때 유머러스하게 표현

patterns.pattern: Sometimes, it's almost [adjective].
patterns.explanation: 짧고 인상적인 코멘트로 청중의 웃음이나 공감을 유도하는 패턴.
examples: Sometimes, it's almost magical.
Sometimes, it's almost scary how accurate it is.

drillQuestions.question: Sometimes, it's _____ creepy.

sentences.notes: 의도적으로 짧은 문장으로 유머 효과. 기술 강연에서 긴 설명 사이에 짧은 감상을 넣어 청중의 긴장을 풀어주는 기법. creepy는 구어적 표현으로, "불쾌하게 정확한"이라는 뉘앙스.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 유머(humor) — 짧은 한 문장으로 긴장을 풀고 청중의 공감을 유도

sentences.original: These are algorithms that have learned how to do this from data rather than being programmed by hand.
sentences.translation: 이것들은 손으로 프로그래밍된 것이 아니라, 데이터로부터 이것을 하는 법을 배운 알고리즘입니다.

grammarElements.text: that have learned how to do this from data
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (that — algorithms를 수식)

grammarElements.text: rather than being programmed by hand
grammarElements.label: 대조구 (rather than + 동명사 수동태)

vocabulary.example: The dress was made by hand.
We need more data to make a decision.

expressions.phrase: learned how to do this from data
expressions.usage: 머신러닝의 핵심 원리를 간결하게 설명

expressions.phrase: rather than being programmed by hand
expressions.usage: 전통적 방식과의 대비를 표현. rather than + 동명사

patterns.pattern: These are [things] that have learned [how to verb] from [source] rather than being [past participle]
patterns.explanation: 머신러닝의 핵심 개념을 대조를 통해 설명하는 패턴. 학습 vs 프로그래밍.
examples: These are systems that have learned how to translate from examples rather than being given rules.

drillQuestions.question: These algorithms learned from data _____ than being programmed.
They were not programmed by _____.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 머신러닝의 핵심 정의를 담고 있다: "데이터로부터 배운다" vs "수작업으로 프로그래밍된다". rather than + being(동명사 수동태)는 대비를 만드는 고급 구조.

koreanAlignment.en: that have learned how to do this
rather than being programmed by hand

pronunciationNotes: data: /ˈdeɪtə/ 또는 /ˈdætə/ — 미국에서는 "데이타" 또는 "대타" 둘 다 사용
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 대조(contrast) — "학습" vs "프로그래밍"의 대비로 머신러닝의 패러다임 전환을 강조

sentences.original: This is also how IBM was successful in getting Watson to beat the two world champions at "Jeopardy," answering incredibly subtle and complex questions like this one.
sentences.translation: 이것은 또한 IBM이 Watson을 통해 "Jeopardy" 퀴즈쇼에서 두 명의 세계 챔피언을 이기는 데 성공한 방법이기도 합니다. 이처럼 놀라울 정도로 미묘하고 복잡한 질문에 답하면서요.

grammarElements.text: also how IBM was successful in getting Watson to beat the two world champions
grammarElements.label: 보어 (how절)

grammarElements.text: answering incredibly subtle and complex questions
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (동시동작)

vocabulary.example: The difference is very subtle.
The problem is more complex than it seems.
The project was very successful.

expressions.usage: be successful in + 동명사로 성공한 행위를 설명
두 형용사를 and로 연결하여 난이도를 강조

patterns.pattern: This is also how [subject] was successful in getting [object] to [verb] ...
patterns.explanation: 이전에 설명한 원리가 다른 사례에도 적용됨을 보여주는 전환 패턴.
examples: This is also how SpaceX was successful in getting rockets to land themselves.

drillQuestions.question: IBM was successful _____ getting Watson to win.
Watson answered incredibly _____ and complex questions.

sentences.notes: "be successful in + 동명사"는 "~하는 데 성공하다"라는 의미. subtle의 b는 묵음이다. Jeopardy는 미국의 유명 퀴즈쇼로, 2011년 IBM의 Watson이 인간 챔피언을 이겨 큰 화제가 되었다.

koreanAlignment.en: IBM was successful in getting Watson
to beat the two world champions
koreanAlignment.ko: 두 명의 세계 챔피언을 이기는 데

koreanAlignment.en: answering incredibly subtle and complex questions
koreanAlignment.ko: 놀라울 정도로 미묘하고 복잡한 질문에 답하면서

sentences.original: This is also why we are now able to see the first self-driving cars.
sentences.translation: 이것은 또한 우리가 최초의 자율주행 자동차를 볼 수 있게 된 이유이기도 합니다.

grammarElements.text: also why we are now able to see
grammarElements.label: 보어 (why절)

vocabulary.example: Self-driving cars are becoming more common.

expressions.usage: 이전 논점이 또 다른 사례에도 적용됨을 연결
새롭게 가능해진 것을 강조

patterns.pattern: This is also why we are now able to [verb] ...
patterns.explanation: 머신러닝의 응용 사례를 연쇄적으로 소개하는 전환 패턴.
examples: This is also why we are now able to translate languages in real time.
This is also why we are now able to diagnose diseases earlier.

drillQuestions.question: This is also _____ we can see self-driving cars.
We are now _____ to see the first self-driving cars.

sentences.notes: "This is also why"와 이전 문장의 "This is also how"는 같은 구조의 변형으로, 병렬적 전개를 만든다. self-driving은 self-(자기) + driving(운전)의 합성 형용사.

koreanAlignment.en: we are now able to see
koreanAlignment.ko: 우리가 이제 볼 수 있게 된

sentences.original: We don't know how to write those programs by hand, but with machine learning, this is now possible.
sentences.translation: 우리는 그런 프로그램을 수작업으로 작성하는 방법을 모릅니다. 하지만 머신러닝으로, 이것은 이제 가능합니다.

grammarElements.text: how to write those programs by hand
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (의문사 + to부정사)

vocabulary.example: This sweater was knitted by hand.
Is it possible to finish today?

expressions.usage: 인간의 능력 한계를 솔직하게 인정할 때

expressions.phrase: but with machine learning, this is now possible
expressions.usage: 기술이 불가능을 가능으로 바꾸었음을 강조

patterns.pattern: We don't know how to [verb] ..., but with [technology], this is now possible.
patterns.explanation: 인간의 한계를 인정하고, 기술이 이를 해결함을 대비하는 핵심 패턴.
examples: We don't know how to predict earthquakes perfectly, but with AI, this is now possible.
We don't know how to read ancient scripts, but with deep learning, this is now possible.

drillQuestions.question: We don't know _____ to write those programs.
But _____ machine learning, this is now possible.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 강연의 핵심 메시지 중 하나: 인간이 명시적으로 규칙을 정의할 수 없는 것을 머신러닝이 데이터로부터 학습한다. "how to write"는 의문사 + to부정사로 "방법"을 의미.

koreanAlignment.en: We don't know how to write those programs by hand
koreanAlignment.ko: 우리는 그런 프로그램을 수작업으로 작성하는 방법을 모릅니다

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 대비(antithesis) — 인간의 한계와 기술의 가능성을 극적으로 대비하여 머신러닝의 가치를 부각

sentences.original: And in fact, this car has driven over a million miles without any accidents on regular roads.
sentences.translation: 그리고 사실, 이 자동차는 일반 도로에서 사고 없이 100만 마일 이상을 운행했습니다.

vocabulary.example: She has driven this route hundreds of times.
There was a car accident on the highway.
This is a regular classroom.

expressions.usage: 큰 숫자로 인상적인 성과를 강조
안전성을 강조하는 표현. without any는 완전한 부재를 나타냄

patterns.pattern: [Subject] has [past participle] over [number] [unit] without any [negative noun]
patterns.explanation: 현재완료로 누적 성과를 제시하고 without으로 안전성을 강조하는 패턴.
examples: The airline has flown over 10 million passengers without any incidents.
The factory has produced over a million units without any defects.

drillQuestions.question: This car _____ driven over a million miles.
It drove _____ any accidents.

sentences.notes: 현재완료 "has driven"은 과거부터 현재까지의 누적 성과를 나타낸다. "a million miles"는 약 160만 km. "regular roads"는 테스트 트랙이 아닌 실제 일반 도로라는 점을 강조하여 기술의 실용성을 부각.
